channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rescue efforts from Myanmar's deadly earthquake wind down as death toll exceeds 3,500
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Long-shot efforts to find survivors from Myanmar’s devastating March 28 earthquake were winding down Monday, as rescue efforts were supplanted by increasing relief and recovery activity, with the death toll from the disaster surpassing 3,500 and still climbing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pakistani security forces kill 9 militants in a raid near the Afghan border
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani security forces raided a militant hideout in the restive northwest near the Afghan border, killing nine militants, the military said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Swollen rivers flood towns in US South after dayslong deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Kentucky (AP) — Days of unrelenting heavy rain and storms that killed at least 18 people worsened flooding as some rivers rose to near-record levels and inundated towns across an already saturated U.S. South and parts of the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: JON CHERRY, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family says vehicle hit by lightning on highway sounded like a gunshot
item.description: An Illinois family's vehicle was struck by lightning while driving on a highway, which they described to deputies as sounding like a gunshot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rising rivers threaten US South and Midwest after dayslong torrent of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Kentucky (AP) — Rivers rose and flooding worsened Sunday across the sodden U.S. South and Midwest, threatening communities already badly damaged by days of heavy rain and wind that killed at least 18 people.
item.dc:creator: JON CHERRY, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The link between truancy, violent crime and how Louisiana is working to break
item.description: "Truants are often the children of parents who did not find success in their own school or value education.
item.dc:creator: Marlo Lacen, NewsNation

item.title: Houston, Florida play for title and put a different spin on the underdog role to wrap March Madness
item.description: Coach Kelvin Sampson’s squad of defenders and deniers face Florida for the national title Monday night. They wrap up a front-runner’s Final Four that featured all No. 1 seeds but ends with the two top ones — Auburn and Duke — sitting at home.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson strikes deal with Rep. Luna on parental proxy voting
item.description: This marks a development that is set to unlock the House floor after the explosive issue — and the GOP rebellions it sparked — brought the chamber to a standstill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: Johnson strikes deal with Luna on parental proxy voting
item.description: Under the agreement being worked out, the House would formalize “vote pairing,” two sources familiar with the matter confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge calls Trump's deportation of Maryland man 'wholly lawless' in scathing decision
item.description: A federal judge, in a scathing decision on Sunday, said the Trump administration had no legal grounds to arrest, detain, and deport a Salvadoran national living in Maryland to a prison in his home country, saying the decision was “wholly lawless.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Researchers developing at-home smell test to detect Alzheimer’s
item.description: A smell test is less invasive and more accessible than other methods like PET scans, lumbar punctures or blood tests.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrants charged in Nike shoe heist as cartels rob US cargo trains
item.description: Police in Arizona arrested two illegal immigrants in the latest case of Nike shoes being stolen from cargo trains traveling through the Mojave Desert.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Second child died from measles-related causes in West Texas
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stellantis joins Ford in offering employee discounts to public
item.description: The move follows in the steps of Ford Motor Company, which announced a similar bargain earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KTLA 5

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding, tornadoes as storms slash parts of US
item.description: Many of the impacted areas already are heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that spawned deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Most cars have this button, but few know when to use it
item.description: An often overlooked button on your car or truck's dashboard could be saving you some sweat and money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida makes push all the way to NCAA title game, set to face Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida has gone from being picked to finish sixth in the rugged Southeastern Conference to pushing all the way to the final Monday night of the season. Now the Gators face a Houston team that just pushes teams around with its suffocating defense.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Parts of the South and Midwest, still reeling from violent storms, tornadoes and flooding that have killed more than a dozen people, face new flooding and tornado warnings that forecasters said could last for days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP senators back crackdown on foreign students
item.description: Republican senators are brushing off concerns that high-profile arrests and deportations of foreign students may ostracize a group that is a major contributor to the U.S. economy. International students injected $43.8 billion into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding and tornadoes as storms slash from Texas to Ohio
item.description: DYERSBURG, Tennessee. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding came Saturday for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned deadly tornadoes. Forecasters warned that rivers in some places would continue to rise for days.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and OBED LAMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover maker pauses shipments to US as it develops post-tariff plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The maker of Jaguar and Land Rover cars is pausing shipments to the U.S. as Britain's struggling auto industry begins to respond to the 25% tax on vehicle imports imposed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amanda Knox gives warning to students wanting to study abroad, 10 years after being acquitted of murder
item.description: Amanda Knox, a married mother of two, gives advice to students and their parents on whether study abroad is a safe option in 2025. She has a new book, "Free: My Search for Meaning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California culinary star dies after hiking in Hawaii
item.description: Gianna Buzzetta was killed in a freak accident in Kauai, Hawaii, while hiking with her boyfriend last week.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs will make sneakers, jeans and almost everything Americans wear cost more, trade groups warn
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sending children back to school in new sneakers, jeans and T-shirts is likely to cost U.S. families significantly more this fall if the bespoke tariffs President Donald Trump put on leading exporters take effect as planned, American industry groups warn.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Storms tore up two of America's most iconic trails. Federal cuts have disrupted repairs
item.description: CAMPO, Calif. (AP) — Hiking the Pacific Crest Trail is a challenge, especially for adventurers making the entire run from Southern California to Canada, and Eric Kipperman’s job is to greet them at the start and lay bare the difficulties ahead.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clayton leads Florida to NCAA title game, scoring 34 points in 79-73 victory over SEC rival Auburn
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Walter Clayton Jr. is having an NCAA Tournament for the ages. Florida now just needs him to deliver one more time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US must return Maryland father mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Protesters tee off against Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies across the US, Southern California
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Are Trump’s tariffs a negotiating tool? Depends on whom you ask
item.description: Businesses, consumers and foreign leaders are trying to assess just how set in stone President Trump’s tariffs are, as the administration and its allies send mixed signals about whether the measures are being used for leverage.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Actor Glen Powell launches organic condiments line
item.description: Actor and producer Glen Powell has launched Smash Kitchen, a line of organic condiments that includes non-GMO pantry essentials with remixed flavors, now exclusively available nationwide at Walmart stores and Walmart.com.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, Fox 5

item.title: JP Morgan predicts 2025 recession as Trump tariffs rattle market
item.description: President Trump's tariffs and their fallout will cause the U.S. economy to enter a recession this year, J.P. Morgan predicts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration issues demands on Harvard as conditions for billions in federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has issued a list of demands Harvard University must meet as a condition for receiving almost $9 billion in grants and contracts, federal money that is being threatened during an investigation into campus antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
